description.p: Eventix is an iCalendar event and task manager for Linux desktops. It runs a local web server that provides a calendar UI, and ships a GTK desktop wrapper that embeds that UI in a native application window with system tray integration.

ul.li: Updated dependencies.
Fixed bug in attendee adding.
Fixed bug in edit following occurrences.
Fixed bug in making non-recurrent items recurrent.
Fixed bug in edit form for all-day events.
Fixed bug in display of DST gaps/folds.
Remember scroll position in weekly view.
Handle resize failures more gracefully.
Remember window size and maximized state.
Fixed bug in next-tasks ordering.
Fixed bugs in UTC handling.
Added screenshots.
Added line information to parse errors in ical crate.
Fixed bug in flatpak image creation.
Renamed Application ID to io.github.hrniels.Eventix (required for FlatHub).
Reworked flatpak packaging to prepare for FlatHub.
Flatpak packaging improvements.
Remember maximized state for Wayland.
VTIMEZONE Support: Added full support for reading, writing, and utilizing VTIMEZONE in ical and eventix.
Background Synchronization: Sync operations now run in the background, including a 5-minute auto-sync after user activity.
Calendar Management: Added ability to add and delete calendars from remote collections (e.g., Radicale, O365).
Desktop Keyring Integration: Built-in support for retrieving passwords from org.freedesktop.secrets (desktop-keyring).
Drag & Drop / Resizing: Support for resizing events in the weekly view and event copying via Ctrl + Drag.
DST Awareness: Added visualization of DST gaps and folds in the weekly view; improved occurrences generation and duration calculations to account for DST changes.
Enhanced Recurrence Options: Added "nth day" (e.g., 2nd Tuesday) option for monthly repeats and expanded yearly repetition options.
Sync Window Setting: Added a configurable sync time span window for collections.
Auto-migration: Added auto-migration for TOML configuration files to newer versions.
Improved UI/UX: Dynamically resizing monthly/weekly tables, custom confirm/alert dialogs, keybindings enabled when no input is focused, and spinning icons for long-running operations.
RFC Conformance: Numerous fixes for EXDATE, UNTIL, SEQUENCE, and VALARM to better align with iCalendar specifications.
Error Handling: Added UI for AJAX errors and better handling of failed requests.
Dependencies: Updated core dependencies including gtk, webkit, askama, and serde_qs.
